commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: linen: This he himself explained above to be "the righteousness of saints;" as it is said by the Psalmist, "Let thy priests be clothed with righteousness." <a

commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: armies: That is to say, the Church in white bodies imitated Him. For because of the intensity of its conflict, it rightly receives the name of an army.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: The armies The celestial armies of saints follow the Son of God, as their captain and commander; they are all like him, mounted on white horses, and clothed in fine linen, white and clean, a symbol of their merit and glory. (Pastorini)
